summary:
Frontend e2e testing of role-specific dashboards (iteration 4). 7 acceptance criteria PASS, 1 FAIL. Verified on live preview URL: (1) HOD (dinesh.80@hod.com) -> /staff/home/ renders hod_dashboard.html with heading 'Holidays from 2083/02/15 to 2084/03/32' and devanagari rows (गणतन्त्र दिवस, पूर्णिमाव्रत, अनला पुन्हिः etc.) - Nepali calendar intact. (2) Store Manager (store@staff.com) -> NEW dashboard with title 'Store Manager Dashboard', user header 'Bishal Maharjan / Store Manager / Inventory & Store Operations', all 4 KPI cards present (card-total-items=5, card-low-stock=3, card-pending-reqs=2, plus Total Quantity=70), Low Stock Items table + Recent Requisitions table both render. 'Manage Inventory' link href=/modules/inventory/ and 'All Requisitions' href=/store/requisitions/ both correct. (3) Accountant (accountant@staff.com) -> NEW 'Accountant Dashboard' with 'Sabina Pradhan / Accountant / Finance & Payroll', 4 KPI cards (card-total-payslips=4, card-paid=4, card-pending-payslips=0, card-payroll=248000.00), Recent Payslips table with 4 PDF buttons per row, Quick Actions panel (Generate New Payslip/Staff Directory/Review Leave Requests/Notifications). data-testid='accountant-header' present. (4) Librarian (librarian@staff.com) -> NEW 'Library Admin Dashboard' with 'Sujan Bhattarai / Librarian / Library Administration', 4 KPI cards (card-total-books=3, card-active-loans=1, card-overdue=0, plus Total Returned=0), Active Loans table + Recent Returns table render, 'Manage Library' link href=/library/manage/ correct. (5) Teacher (staff@staff.com) -> /staff/home/ renders home_content.html (no Nepali calendar, no role-specific dashboard markers - distinct from HOD). (6) Admin (admin@sagarmatha.edu) -> /admin/home/ (distinct admin UI). (7) Student (student@student.com) -> /student/home/ (distinct student UI). All 4 staff role-specific dashboards correctly do NOT render the Nepali holiday calendar (only HOD does). All data-testid markers ('store-mgr-header', 'accountant-header', 'library-admin-header', card-*) verified present. NO 500 errors on any role landing.

FAIL: Accountant PDF download returns HTTP 403 Forbidden. The accountant dashboard exposes 4 'PDF' buttons (one per recent payslip), all linking to /selfservice/payslip/<id>/pdf/. Fetching /selfservice/payslip/3/pdf/ with accountant session cookies returns 403 with body 'Forbidden' (9 bytes, text/html). Root cause: main_app/extra_views.py::payslip_pdf line 564-571 only allows the payslip's own staff, HOD role (role.startswith('hod')), or admin (user_type=1) - the Accountant role is NOT whitelisted. This contradicts review spec: 'Clicking a PDF button on a payslip row downloads a real application/pdf binary.'

critical.issue: Returns 403 Forbidden when requested by an Accountant user. Authorization check in payslip_pdf (extra_views.py:561-571) only allows payslip owner, HOD role, or admin (user_type=1). Accountant role must be added to the whitelist so the Accountant Dashboard's PDF buttons actually work.

integration_issues.issue: Dashboard exposes PDF buttons but backend returns 403. Either grant Accountant access in payslip_pdf, or remove the PDF buttons from the accountant dashboard.

action_items: Fix payslip_pdf authorization in /app/main_app/extra_views.py:564-571 to also allow Accountant role (e.g., add: or _is_accountant_role(requester_staff.role)). Without this, the Accountant Dashboard's primary action (download payslip PDFs) is broken.
(Optional but recommended) Consider whether Library Admin / Store Manager need any payslip access; currently they do not have PDF buttons so no action required, but be consistent.
critical_code_review_comments: payslip_pdf authorization is hard-coded with role.startswith('hod') and user_type check; should use the same _is_*_role helpers used by staff_views.py dispatch (DRY). The new role-detection helpers should be centralized in a single roles.py module so dashboard dispatch and view-level authorization stay in sync.

Accountant dashboard exposes a 'PDF' button per row even when the requesting user has no permission - the UI should not render an action the backend will refuse. Either (a) backend grants Accountant access (recommended per spec) or (b) template hides the PDF button for unauthorized users.

All 4 new role dashboards share the same base chrome (sidebar + Modules dropdown). Worth confirming the sidebar menu items shown for Store Manager / Accountant / Librarian are role-appropriate (e.g., a Librarian sidebar showing 'Add Result / Edit Results / Take Attendance' is academically-oriented and may not match their role).

success_rate.frontend: 87.5% (7/8 acceptance criteria pass; accountant PDF download fails with 403)

test_credentials: Admin admin@sagarmatha.edu/admin123, HOD dinesh.80@hod.com/dinesh.80, Teacher staff@staff.com/staff123, Store store@staff.com/store123, Accountant accountant@staff.com/accountant123, Librarian librarian@staff.com/librarian123, Student student@student.com/student123. All seeded by `python manage.py seed_demo`.
seed_data_creation: Ran `python manage.py seed_demo` which re-creates the 3 new users (Bishal Maharjan/Sabina Pradhan/Sujan Bhattarai) plus pre-existing roles. Seed is idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 7 role dashboards render with correct headers, KPI cards, and data-testid markers. The only failing acceptance criterion is the Accountant->PDF download (403). After main agent patches payslip_pdf authorization, re-verify by: login as accountant@staff.com, GET /selfservice/payslip/3/pdf/ via page.context.request.get(), assert status==200 and Content-Type=='application/pdf' and body[:4]==b'%PDF'. Useful selectors verified: [data-testid='store-mgr-header'], [data-testid='accountant-header'], [data-testid='library-admin-header'], [data-testid='card-total-items'], [data-testid='card-low-stock'], [data-testid='card-pending-reqs'], [data-testid='card-total-payslips'], [data-testid='card-paid'], [data-testid='card-pending-payslips'], [data-testid='card-payroll'], [data-testid='card-total-books'], [data-testid='card-active-loans'], [data-testid='card-overdue'].
rca of the issue: BUG: Accountant payslip PDF download returns 403. REPRODUCE: (1) login as accountant@staff.com/accountant123, (2) on /staff/home/ click any 'PDF' button in the Recent Payslips table, OR programmatically fetch /selfservice/payslip/3/pdf/ with the accountant's session. EXPECTED: HTTP 200, Content-Type=application/pdf, body starts with %PDF. ACTUAL: HTTP 403, body='Forbidden'. ROOT CAUSE: main_app/extra_views.py::payslip_pdf (lines 561-571) authorization gate: `if (requester_staff is None) or (payslip.staff_id != requester_staff.id and (requester_staff.role or '').strip().lower().startswith('hod') is False): if request.user.user_type != '1': return HttpResponse('Forbidden', status=403)`. The Accountant role string does not startswith 'hod' and accountants are user_type=2, so they get rejected on every payslip that is not their own. MITIGATION: extend the allow-list to include Accountant role, e.g. `role_lower = (requester_staff.role or '').lower(); is_privileged = role_lower.startswith('hod') or 'accountant' in role_lower`. Same _is_accountant_role helper from staff_views.py should be reused.
